 {"id":"bd-1wc","title":"Implement CLI E2E integration tests with verbose logging","description":"# Implement CLI E2E integration tests with verbose logging

## Goal
Create end-to-end tests that invoke the pi binary directly and verify behavior,
with detailed logging for debugging failures.

## Background
src/main.rs (1581 lines) and src/cli.rs (332 lines) have minimal testing.
The CLI is the primary user interface and needs comprehensive E2E tests.

## Scope Boundary (Important)
- This bead builds the CLI E2E harness + offline CLI flag coverage.
- Provider-dependent scenarios live in:
  - bd-1ub (print mode + stdin)
  - bd-idw (session lifecycle)
  - bd-1o4 (tool enable/disable + error paths)

## Approach

### Test Harness
```rust
// tests/e2e_cli.rs

use std::process::{Command, Stdio};
use std::time::Instant;

struct CliTestHarness {
    binary_path: PathBuf,
    temp_dir: TempDir,
    env: HashMap<String, String>,
    logs: Vec<LogEntry>,
}

struct LogEntry {
    timestamp: Instant,
    level: LogLevel,
    message: String,
    context: HashMap<String, String>,
}

impl CliTestHarness {
    fn new() -> Self {
        let binary = cargo_bin("pi");
        let temp = TempDir::new().unwrap();
        Self {
            binary_path: binary,
            temp_dir: temp,
            env: HashMap::new(),
            logs: Vec::new(),
        }
    }

    fn log(&mut self, level: LogLevel, msg: &str, ctx: &[(&str, &str)]) {
        self.logs.push(LogEntry {
            timestamp: Instant::now(),
            level,
            message: msg.to_string(),
            context: ctx.iter().map(|(k,v)| (k.to_string(), v.to_string())).collect(),
        });
    }

    fn run(&mut self, args: &[&str]) -> CliResult {
        self.log(Info, "Starting CLI", &[("args", &args.join(" "))]);

        let start = Instant::now();
        let output = Command::new(&self.binary_path)
            .args(args)
            .envs(&self.env)
            .current_dir(self.temp_dir.path())
            .output()
            .expect("Failed to run pi");

        let duration = start.elapsed();
        self.log(Info, "CLI completed", &[
            ("exit_code", &output.status.code().unwrap_or(-1).to_string()),
            ("duration_ms", &duration.as_millis().to_string()),
            ("stdout_len", &output.stdout.len().to_string()),
            ("stderr_len", &output.stderr.len().to_string()),
        ]);

        CliResult {
            exit_code: output.status.code().unwrap_or(-1),
            stdout: String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout).to_string(),
            stderr: String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr).to_string(),
            duration,
        }
    }

    fn dump_logs(&self) -> String {
        // Format all logs for debugging
    }
}
```

## Logging Requirements
- Use TestLogger (bd-3ml) in the harness so failures auto-dump logs.
- Log: command, env (redacted), cwd, duration, stdout/stderr snapshot, and artifact paths.
- Attach logs + outputs as artifacts on failure (text snapshots).

## Determinism Requirements
- Offline-only tests here (help/version/list/config). Any provider-dependent test must live in sub-beads and use VCR playback.
- Ensure environment is fully controlled (PI_CONFIG_PATH, PI_SESSIONS_DIR).

## Test Categories (Offline)

### 1. Basic Invocation Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_version_flag() {
    let mut harness = CliTestHarness::new();
    let result = harness.run(&["--version"]);
    assert_eq!(result.exit_code, 0);
    assert!(result.stdout.contains("pi "));
    assert!(result.duration < Duration::from_millis(100));
}

#[test]
fn test_help_flag() {
    let mut harness = CliTestHarness::new();
    let result = harness.run(&["--help"]);
    assert_eq!(result.exit_code, 0);
    assert!(result.stdout.contains("Usage:"));
}

#[test]
fn test_invalid_flag() {
    let mut harness = CliTestHarness::new();
    let result = harness.run(&["--invalid-flag"]);
    assert_ne!(result.exit_code, 0);
    assert!(result.stderr.contains("error"));
}
```

### 2. Model Listing Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_list_models() {
    let mut harness = CliTestHarness::new();
    let result = harness.run(&["--list-models"]);
    assert_eq!(result.exit_code, 0);
    assert!(result.stdout.contains("claude"));
}

#[test]
fn test_list_models_filter() {
    let mut harness = CliTestHarness::new();
    let result = harness.run(&["--list-models", "opus"]);
    assert_eq!(result.exit_code, 0);
    assert!(result.stdout.contains("opus"));
    assert!(!result.stdout.contains("sonnet"));
}
```

### 3. Subcommand Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_list_packages() {
    let mut harness = CliTestHarness::new();
    let result = harness.run(&["list"]);
    assert_eq!(result.exit_code, 0);
}

#[test]
fn test_config_subcommand() {
    let mut harness = CliTestHarness::new();
    let result = harness.run(&["config"]);
    // Verify output shows config paths
}
```

## Logging Format
```
[2026-02-02T10:30:45.123Z] INFO  Starting CLI
    args: --version
[2026-02-02T10:30:45.135Z] INFO  CLI completed
    exit_code: 0
    duration_ms: 12
    stdout_len: 23
    stderr_len: 0
--- STDOUT ---
pi 0.1.0
--- STDERR ---
(empty)
```

## Files
- tests/e2e_cli.rs
- tests/e2e_cli/harness.rs
- tests/e2e_cli/logging.rs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test harness with verbose logging
- [ ] Offline CLI flags tested (version/help/list/config)
- [ ] Logs dumped on test failure
- [ ] Tests run in <30 seconds total

 {"id":"bd-20c","title":"Migrate core runtime to asupersync (agent/tools/session)","description":"Background:
- Provider HTTP migration (bd-1xf) is necessary but not sufficient to remove tokio.
- Core runtime still uses tokio for agent loop, channels, tool execution, and session IO.

Scope / Steps:
1) Replace tokio runtime bootstrap in main.rs with asupersync RuntimeBuilder + reactor.
2) Migrate agent loop + tool execution to Cx-based async; replace tokio channels with asupersync equivalents.
3) Ensure session IO + indexing use asupersync-friendly APIs (no tokio traits).
4) Update tests to use LabRuntime for deterministic time/cancellation.
5) Remove tokio dependency once all call sites migrated.

Acceptance:
- pi runs end-to-end without tokio in Cargo.toml.
- Cancellation + cleanup behave correctly across agent/tool/session paths.
- Tests pass under LabRuntime.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:02:00.867634353Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T17:02:00.878278870Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-20c","depends_on_id":"bd-1xf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:02:00.878263110Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-20c","depends_on_id":"bd-2qk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:02:00.874622706Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-26s","title":"Workstream: Comprehensive Test Coverage (No Mocks)","description":"# Workstream: Comprehensive Test Coverage (No Mocks)

## Goal
Achieve 95%+ test coverage using real implementations wherever possible, with
detailed logging for E2E integration tests. Eliminate reliance on mocks for
core functionality testing.

## Background
Current test coverage analysis reveals critical gaps:

### Well Tested (Good)
- Built-in Tools: 100% coverage (122 conformance fixture cases)
- Session Tree Logic: 11 tests covering branching/navigation
- SSE Parser: 9 tests covering all edge cases
- Auth Flow: 3 tests for PKCE/OAuth setup

### Critical Gaps (Bad)
- **Provider Streaming**: 0 tests with real API/streaming (only unit tests)
- **Interactive TUI**: 2 tests out of 2842 lines (<0.1%)
- **CLI/main.rs**: 0 tests for 1581 lines
- **Compaction**: 0 tests for 903 lines
- **Configuration**: 0 tests for 332 lines
- **Session Persistence**: Only serialization tested, not full I/O cycle

### What "No Mocks" Means
- Provider tests: Use VCR-style recorded responses, not fake providers
- File tests: Use real temp directories (already done)
- CLI tests: Use actual binary execution
- TUI tests: Use terminal emulator or snapshot testing
- Network tests: Use local test server or recorded responses

## Testing Strategy

### 1. Provider Streaming Tests (VCR Style)
Record real API responses and replay them:
- No external network calls during CI
- Real response parsing (not simplified mocks)
- Error scenarios from real API errors

### 2. E2E Integration Tests
Full workflow tests with verbose logging:
- Start pi binary with arguments
- Verify output and exit codes
- Check session files created
- Log every step for debugging

### 3. Snapshot Testing for TUI
Capture terminal output and compare:
- Render TUI components to string
- Compare against golden snapshots
- Update snapshots on intentional changes

### 4. Property-Based Testing
Use proptest for edge cases:
- Truncation with random inputs
- Session serialization round-trips
- Path resolution edge cases

### 5. Compaction Tests
- Window selection + summary insertion
- Tool/thinking/image content handling
- Session invariants preserved after compaction

### 6. Configuration Tests
- Config discovery + precedence rules
- Env + CLI override behavior
- Invalid config errors with actionable messages

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Provider streaming tested with recorded responses
- [ ] TUI logic tested (state transitions, rendering)
- [ ] CLI argument parsing fully tested
- [ ] Compaction logic tested
- [ ] Configuration loading tested
- [ ] Session persistence cycles tested
- [ ] E2E tests with verbose logging exist
- [ ] CI runs all tests in <5 minutes

## Files to Create
- tests/provider_streaming.rs - VCR-style provider tests
- tests/e2e_cli.rs - CLI integration tests
- tests/tui_snapshot.rs - TUI snapshot tests
- tests/compaction.rs - Compaction pipeline tests
- tests/config_loading.rs - Config discovery/override tests
- tests/fixtures/vcr/*.json - Recorded API responses

## Dependencies
None (can start immediately, independent of HTTP migration)","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:34:05.966405126Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T05:46:20.507382886Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-26s","depends_on_id":"bd-2qk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:34:05.973753960Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2hz","title":"Extension UI protocol integration (interactive + RPC)","description":"Background:
- Legacy spec defines extension_ui_request/extension_ui_response (confirm/select/input/editor/notify/etc.) and blocking semantics.
- EXTENSIONS.md mandates Node/Bun-free runtime with capability-gated connectors and deterministic logs.

Scope / Steps:
1) Implement runtime plumbing so extension UI requests surface in interactive TUI (prompt/confirm/select/editor) with timeout + cancellation.
2) Implement RPC mode passthrough: emit extension_ui_request on stdout; accept extension_ui_response on stdin.
3) Enforce request/response ID mapping, default values, and timeouts; normalize errors.
4) Add conformance fixtures for each UI method + timeout/cancel edge cases; wire into tests.
5) Emit structured logs (pi.ext.log.v1) for UI requests/responses with correlation IDs.

Acceptance:
- A sample extension can invoke confirm/select/input/editor and receive responses in both interactive + RPC modes.
- Timeouts and cancellation propagate correctly without deadlocks.
- Fixtures pass and logs are deterministic.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:01:36.808698612Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T17:01:36.834579243Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2hz","depends_on_id":"bd-1uy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:01:36.822115496Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2hz","depends_on_id":"bd-2i5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:01:36.825381412Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2hz","depends_on_id":"bd-3so","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:01:36.834563744Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2hz","depends_on_id":"bd-576","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:01:36.830999362Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
@@ -87,7 +87,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-3nz","title":"Wire session indexing into session.persist()","description":"# Wire session indexing into session.persist()

## Goal
Automatically update the SQLite index when sessions are saved.

## Implementation

### Add Index Update to persist()
```rust
// src/session.rs

impl Session {
    pub async fn persist(&self, path: &Path) -> Result<()> {
        // Existing JSONL write logic
        self.write_jsonl(path).await?;
        
        // NEW: Update index
        if let Ok(index) = SessionIndex::open_default() {
            if let Err(e) = index.index_session(path) {
                // Log warning but don't fail persist
                tracing::warn!("Failed to update session index: {}", e);
            }
        }
        
        Ok(())
    }
}
```

### Default Index Path
```rust
// src/session_index.rs

impl SessionIndex {
    pub fn open_default() -> Result<Self> {
        let path = dirs::data_dir()
            .unwrap_or_else(|| PathBuf::from("."))
            .join(".pi/agent/sessions/index.db");
        Self::open(&path)
    }
}
```

### Handle Index Creation
- Create index.db if not exists
- Use WAL mode for concurrent access
- Handle permission errors gracefully

## Considerations
1. **Performance**: Index update should be fast (<10ms)
2. **Reliability**: Don't fail persist if index fails
3. **Concurrency**: Handle multiple pi instances
4. **Migration**: Handle existing sessions without index entries

## Testing
- Test: persist() updates index
- Test: persist() succeeds even if index fails
- Test: Index contains session after persist

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] persist() calls index_session()
- [ ] Index created if not exists
- [ ] Errors logged but don't fail persist
- [ ] Performance impact <10ms","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:38:32.093344392Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T17:06:32.438585082Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3nz","depends_on_id":"bd-346","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:38:32.099125014Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":24,"issue_id":"bd-3nz","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Picking up bd-3nz. Plan: make Session::save() update SessionIndex (best-effort: warn-only, never fails save), move index DB under the sessions root for easy isolation, and remove redundant SessionIndex::index_session calls from AgentSession + interactive save paths.
